quick_facts.label: Role definition
quick_facts.value: Corporate Counsel is an in-house legal role: the lawyer advises the employer’s business, manages legal risk, and commonly handles commercial contracts, compliance and transactions rather than serving a portfolio of external clients.

quick_facts.label: Core work varies by company and sector
quick_facts.value: Recent India-based employer postings include drafting, reviewing and negotiating agreements; advising business teams; regulatory and secretarial compliance; transaction support; and management of disputes or external counsel. Technology-sector roles may additionally cover software, cloud, data-protection and cross-border issues.

quick_facts.label: Typical academic foundation
quick_facts.value: An LL.B. is the foundational qualification. For enrolment as an advocate, the Advocates Act requires a law degree from a university recognised for that purpose; BCI legal-education rules recognise both an integrated bachelor’s degree in law and a post-graduation law degree of at least three years, subject to the applicable rules and recognition status.

quick_facts.label: Advocacy and AIBE distinction
quick_facts.value: The Advocates Act reserves practice before courts and authorities to enrolled advocates, while the AIBE is designed to lead to a Certificate of Practice. For an in-house role, employers’ requirements differ: for example, a current India legal-counsel posting lists AIBE and BCI enrolment as preferred rather than mandatory. Candidates should check the exact employer role and whether court representation is expected.

quick_facts.label: Experience expectations are role-level dependent
quick_facts.value: As of July 2026, an Amazon India Associate Corporate Counsel posting sought 3–5 years of post-qualification experience, whereas Wipro’s Bengaluru Corporate Counsel posting sought 8+ years and Salesforce’s India Senior Corporate Counsel posting sought at least 9 years. These are examples, not universal entry requirements.

sections.heading: What Corporate Counsel does
paragraphs: Corporate Counsel translates legal requirements into commercially workable advice for one employer. The work commonly combines contract drafting, review and negotiation with legal-risk advice for business stakeholders. In Wipro’s July 2026 Bengaluru posting, the role supports strategic-partner engagements and requires advice to business teams, negotiation with opposing counsel and protection of the company’s interests.

The portfolio depends strongly on the employer’s industry and size. Amazon’s India Associate Corporate Counsel role includes advisory and contract-management support, local-regulatory and secretarial compliance, and collaboration with business operations. Clariant’s India Legal Counsel role includes commercial agreements, M&A and joint-venture work, agreement management, business-unit advice and coordination of litigation with external counsel. A corporate counsel therefore may be a generalist in a smaller legal team or a specialist in commercial contracting, compliance, privacy, M&A, employment, IP, disputes or another business-critical field.
bullets: Draft, review and negotiate commercial agreements, including NDAs, supply, distribution, software, services, licensing and partner agreements, according to the company’s business model.
Advise internal stakeholders on legal and commercial risk, applicable regulation and the company’s policies; help structure and close transactions.
Maintain compliant processes for contracts and corporate records; depending on the role, support secretarial compliance, investigations, disputes and external-counsel management.
For transaction-focused roles, assist with due diligence, deal documents, approvals, post-closing work, joint ventures, acquisitions, divestments or restructuring.

sections.heading: Entry routes in India
paragraphs: The direct route is an eligible law degree followed by internships and early work that build demonstrable corporate-law skills. The node’s integrated routes—B.A. LL.B. (Hons.), B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.), B.Com. LL.B. (Hons.), B.S.W. LL.B. (Hons.) and B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.)—are examples of five-year integrated law pathways; a three-year LL.B. after a prior bachelor’s degree is another established route. For advocate enrolment, the law degree must meet the Advocates Act and BCI-recognition requirements; candidates should verify the current approval/recognition status of the particular institution rather than assume that a degree title alone is sufficient.

A common early-career route is to work in a law firm, as an in-house legal/contracting professional, or in a legal officer role and then move into a company legal department. This is reflected in current hiring: Amazon’s India Associate Corporate Counsel listing seeks lawyers or in-house attorneys with autonomous responsibility for advising, drafting, reviewing and negotiating commercial contracts, while senior employer postings often seek substantial prior legal experience.

LL.M., an Executive MBA and the Patent Agent Examination can be useful specialisation or progression options, but they are not a universal substitute for the foundational law degree. An LL.M. may deepen a practice area; an Executive MBA can strengthen business and management capability; and the Patent Agent Examination is relevant chiefly where patent practice is central to the intended role.
bullets: Choose a law programme and verify its current BCI recognition/approval status if advocate enrolment or court practice may be part of the plan.
During law school, seek internships in company legal departments, law-firm corporate/M&A or commercial teams, compliance functions, contract-management teams, and sector-specific legal teams.
Build a portfolio of supervised work: contract mark-ups, legal research notes, due-diligence checklists, corporate filings, compliance trackers and concise business-facing advice.
Where the desired job includes practising before courts or authorities, understand the separate advocate-enrolment and AIBE/Certificate-of-Practice pathway.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The central capability is not merely knowing legal rules; it is applying them clearly and pragmatically to a business decision. Employer postings consistently call for strong drafting, negotiation, communication, analytical judgment and stakeholder management. Wipro specifically identifies business acumen, contract drafting and negotiation; Salesforce identifies legal and commercial judgment, cross-functional work and the ability to operate under deadlines.

Specialist knowledge should follow the target sector. A technology-company role may require fluency in software and cloud agreements, data protection, IP, cross-border transactions and procurement. A manufacturing, financial-services or consumer-business role may emphasize supply and distribution contracts, corporate governance, competition, employment, product regulation, disputes or transaction work.
bullets: Commercial contracting: issue spotting, clause drafting, redlining, negotiation strategy, contract interpretation and lifecycle management.
Business partnership: concise risk communication, prioritisation, practical options and collaboration with sales, finance, procurement, HR, security, privacy and leadership.
Legal analysis: statutory and regulatory research, due diligence, compliance assessment, documentation and evidence-based advice.
Professional practice: written and oral communication, confidentiality, attention to detail, ethical judgment, project management and comfort with changing business priorities.

sections.heading: Where and how the role is performed
paragraphs: Corporate Counsel works within a company’s legal department and partners with operational teams rather than functioning solely as external counsel. Current India postings show cross-functional work with sales, pricing, revenue recognition, security, privacy, operations and senior leadership. The job can be based in an office or hybrid, depending on the employer; for example, Clariant’s Airoli legal-counsel posting specifies a hybrid work model.

The pace and subject matter vary. A commercial counsel may support high-volume or high-value customer and vendor contracts; a compliance counsel may design policies, training and controls; and a corporate/M&A counsel may work intensely around deals, due diligence and approvals. Most roles also require careful coordination with outside law firms for litigation, specialist opinions or transactions.
bullets: Employers include Indian companies, multinational subsidiaries, technology and consulting firms, manufacturers, financial-services firms, pharmaceuticals and life-sciences companies, consumer businesses, infrastructure firms and PSUs.
Internal clients commonly include business heads, procurement, sales, HR, finance, company secretarial, security, privacy, product and executive leadership.
External interactions can include customers, suppliers, counterparties, regulators, government bodies and outside counsel.

sections.heading: Career progression
paragraphs: Progression is normally based on the complexity and ownership of matters rather than title alone. An Associate Corporate Counsel may take responsibility for advisory and contract-management work with 3–5 years of post-qualification experience, while Corporate Counsel and Senior Corporate Counsel roles may lead complex negotiations, advise senior stakeholders, own a legal domain and mentor colleagues. The precise ladder varies by employer.

Possible next nodes from this role include Senior Corporate Counsel, Corporate Governance Associate, Legal Manager in a PSU, Law Firm Associate and ultimately General Counsel. An LL.M. or Executive MBA can support a chosen direction, but advancement is usually evidenced through sound legal judgment, trusted stakeholder relationships, ownership of significant matters, and the ability to make legal support scalable through templates, playbooks, training and process improvements.
bullets: Early stage: legal intern, contracts/compliance analyst or junior legal professional; then Associate Corporate Counsel or comparable legal-counsel roles.
Mid-career: Corporate Counsel or specialist counsel with independent ownership of contract, compliance, disputes, privacy, IP, employment or transaction matters.
Senior stage: Senior Corporate Counsel, legal-team lead, head of a specialist legal function, deputy general counsel or General Counsel.
Alternative mobility: move between an in-house team and a law-firm corporate practice, or pursue sector-specific legal roles such as corporate governance, PSU legal management or patent-related work.
